title: T8 dogfood: end-to-end verification on this repo across all 5 scopes
description: Run the rc.22 changes against the Fabric repo itself to catch real-world drift between code-level tests and on-disk reality. Covers: pre-cleanup of stub entries, fab scan migration (B+C), plan-context auto-heal (D), reconcile fix on a re-seeded personal-layer stub (E), final doctor pass.

files.target: leftover stub from prior grill session
files.change: Remove the stub file (no longer needed after rc.22 lands its own dogfood scope-E test)

After `fab scan` runs, verify 4 currently-existing bare-slug baselines (code-style.md, tech-stack.md, module-structure.md, readme-first-paragraph.md) are renamed to id-prefixed form and their frontmatter has tags: []
After a plan-context call, verify the events.jsonl contains a knowledge_meta_auto_healed event entry IF meta was stale (or zero such events if already fresh)

files.target: regression stub for scope-E personal-layer fix
files.change: (1) Re-place a stub at this path with valid frontmatter (id: KP-DEC-9999, scope: broad, type: decision); (2) run fab doctor --fix; (3) verify agents.meta.json now lists KP-DEC-9999; (4) clean up: delete the stub file.

implementation: Run `fab scan` on this repo from project root
Inspect `.fabric/knowledge/{guidelines,models}/`: verify 4 legacy bare-slug files renamed to KT-XXX-NNNN--slug.md form; inspect new files' frontmatter has tags: []
Inspect agents.meta.json: verify content_ref entries use id-prefixed paths
Trigger a plan-context call (e.g., via `fab_plan_context` MCP or a CLI surface that calls planContext) on this repo and inspect events.jsonl tail
If meta was stale, verify a knowledge_meta_auto_healed event was emitted with sane previous + new hashes
If hook is wired in current Claude Code session, verify the '🔄 Fabric: meta auto-refreshed' banner line appears in stderr on next session start
Inspect agents.meta.json — confirm KP-DEC-9999 entry present

Run final `fab doctor` — expect: no errors; possibly demoted agents_meta_stale warning if drift incurred during dogfood (will heal on next read); no `lint-baseline-filename-format` errors (since T5 migrated them)
Record results in a short markdown file at session folder: `${SESSION}/dogfood-results.md`

reference.pattern: Manual repo smoke convention used in prior rc dogfood passes

criteria: 4 legacy bare-slug baseline files renamed in-place to id-prefixed form by single `fab scan` invocation
agents.meta.json content_refs use new id-prefixed paths
Plan-context call emits knowledge_meta_auto_healed event if meta was stale (otherwise zero such events — also a valid pass)
Personal-layer KP-DEC-9999 stub appears in agents.meta.json after `fab doctor --fix`
Final `fab doctor` exits 0 with no errors; warnings (if any) are demoted agents_meta_stale only

rationale.chosen_approach: Repo-self dogfood — runs the actual rc.22 changes against real on-disk state, catches bugs unit tests miss
alternatives_considered: Defer to post-tag stable soak (too late — tag is hard to rewind)
rationale.tradeoffs: Adds manual step before T9 Gemini review (acceptable)

manual_checks: See implementation steps — each step is a manual check
